title: Writing Workshop: Where Every Sentence Gets Judged

sentences.en: Monday. Today's language arts class was a full writing workshop, which meant every single sentence I wrote was about to be examined by classmates.
sentences.zh: 星期一。今天國文課是完整的寫作工作坊，代表我寫的每一句話都即將被同學檢視一遍。

sentences.en: Our teacher told us to spread our drafts out on the desks so partners could read them properly, side by side.
sentences.zh: 老師要我們把草稿攤在桌上，這樣夥伴才能好好並排讀完。

sentences.en: "First, discuss what the writer is trying to do," she said. "Then propose one small way to improve it, not ten big criticisms."
sentences.zh: 「首先，先討論作者想達成什麼，」她說：「然後提出一個小小的改進方式，不要一次丟十個大批評。」

sentences.en: Bernice was my partner, and she immediately proposed that my story's ending felt rushed, arriving too suddenly after such a slow, careful beginning.
sentences.zh: Bernice 是我的夥伴，她馬上提出我故事的結尾感覺太趕，在這麼緩慢又細心的開頭之後，突然就結束了。

sentences.en: I had to admit she had a point, even though my pride wanted to control the conversation and defend every single word I had written.
sentences.zh: 我不得不承認她說得有道理，儘管我的自尊心很想主導整場對話，捍衛我寫的每一個字。

sentences.en: "A striking ending does not need to happen fast," our teacher said, walking between the desks. "It needs to feel earned by everything before it."
sentences.zh: 「一個令人印象深刻的結尾不需要來得快，」老師在課桌之間走動時說：「它需要讓人覺得是被前面的一切鋪陳出來的。」

sentences.en: I tried a new approach: instead of rushing my ending, I let the main character struggle with her decision for one more full paragraph.
sentences.zh: 我試了一個新做法：我沒有急著結束，而是讓主角在做決定前，多掙扎了整整一段。

sentences.en: Our teacher encouraged us to consider how small details influence a reader's opinion of a character, even tiny ones like the color of a character's shoes.
sentences.zh: 老師鼓勵我們思考小細節如何影響讀者對角色的看法，就算只是角色鞋子的顏色這種小事也一樣。

sentences.en: "A good detail does more work than an entire paragraph of explanation," she said, providing an example from a novel we had read earlier this year.
sentences.zh: 「一個好的細節，比一整段解釋做的工作還多，」她說，並舉了我們今年稍早讀過的一本小說當例子。

sentences.en: William's teacher, apparently, is doing something similar with his younger class, though their version mostly involves drawing pictures instead of writing full sentences.
sentences.zh: 顯然 William 的老師也在他們班上做類似的事，只是他們那個版本大部分是畫圖，而不是寫完整的句子。

sentences.en: By the middle of class, I noticed my handwriting had gotten messier the faster I wrote, so I forced myself to slow down and tidy each line.
sentences.zh: 上到一半，我發現我寫得越快，字就越亂，所以我強迫自己放慢速度，把每一行字都寫整齊。

sentences.en: Our teacher gave us fifteen extra minutes when several students asked for a short delay to finish their final paragraphs properly.
sentences.zh: 當好幾個學生要求延遲一點時間，把最後一段好好寫完，老師就多給了我們十五分鐘。

sentences.en: "I want every student to participate honestly in this process," she said, "not just the ones who already feel confident about their writing."
sentences.zh: 她說：「我希望每位學生都能誠實地參與這個過程，」「不只是那些已經對自己寫作很有信心的人。」

sentences.en: Near the end, we had to select one paragraph from our own story to read aloud, which honestly felt scarier than any exam this year.
sentences.zh: 接近尾聲，我們得從自己的故事裡選一段唸出來，老實說，這感覺比今年任何一場考試都還恐怖。

sentences.en: Bernice went first, reading confidently, and her classmates' quiet nodding seemed to satisfy her completely.
sentences.zh: Bernice 先唸，她讀得很有自信，同學安靜點頭的樣子似乎讓她完全滿意。

sentences.en: When it was my turn, my voice shook a little at first, but I settled into a steady rhythm by the second sentence.
sentences.zh: 輪到我的時候，我的聲音一開始有點抖，但唸到第二句就穩定下來了。

sentences.en: Afterward, our teacher said something occurred to her while listening: my quiet, observant writing style actually suits me perfectly, the same way it suits this diary.
sentences.zh: 事後老師說，她在聽的時候突然想到一件事：我這種安靜、善於觀察的寫作風格其實很適合我，就跟這本日記一樣。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: revising a story is not about erasing what you wrote, it is about learning how each small choice operates within the whole piece.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：修改故事不是要把寫過的東西擦掉，而是學習每個小小的選擇，在整篇作品裡是怎麼運作的。

quiz.question: What does Bernice point out about Coco's original ending?
options: It feels rushed after such a slow, careful beginning.
It does not make sense at all.

quiz.question: What does the teacher say a good detail can do?
options: Do more work than an entire paragraph of explanation.
Replace the need for a plot completely.

quiz.question: What does the teacher say about Coco's writing style at the end of class?
options: Her quiet, observant style suits her perfectly.
It needs a complete change of approach.
It is too similar to Bernice's style.
